title: Disposable export editor brief
summary: **Visible export only:** use a disposable HTML editor only when the user must tune a small decision surface and then copy Markdown, JSON, or diff back into the real workflow.

intent.primaryQuestion: When may effective-interact create an editor-like artifact?
intent.decision: Create one only for local, disposable tuning with an explicit export path.
successCriteria: The artifact never writes to the repo, network, credentials, or hidden storage.
Every editable decision can be exported as visible Markdown, JSON, or diff.
The report still has a readable conclusion without interaction.

claims.statement: Editor-like HTML reports are acceptable only when the export path is visible and copyable.
knownLimits: Use frontend-design or web-artifacts-builder when the artifact becomes product UI or a bundled app.

sections.title: Editor boundary
sections.summary: The editor is a decision aid, not a persistence layer.

cards.label: Forbidden
cards.value: no network writes, no repo writes, no credentials, no hidden persistence

sections.title: Export surfaces
sections.summary: Each tab models an explicit export format.

tabs.label: Markdown export
tabs.content: Use for human-readable plans, ticket triage, review notes, and final next actions. The editor should render the resulting Markdown as text that can be copied.

tabs.label: JSON export
tabs.content: Use for small structured choices such as flags, routing rules, or checklist state. The output must be complete enough to paste into a real file after review.

tabs.label: diff export
tabs.content: Use when the safest handoff is a minimal patch. The diff must be visible before the user applies it elsewhere.

sections.title: Safety contract
sections.summary: If one row cannot be satisfied, do not use this skill for the editor.

rows.why: The report is a local decision artifact, not an integration surface.
rows.fallback: Export text and let the user paste or apply it.

rows.why: A disposable editor must not mutate the workspace behind the reader.
The next agent must be able to inspect the full state from the file itself.
rows.fallback: Keep state in visible controls or export blocks.

sections.title: JSON export block
sections.summary: Structured export should be copyable and inert.

sections.title: Diff export preview
sections.summary: A diff export keeps the mutation reviewable outside the report.
sections.content:
--- before.md
+++ after.md
@@ -1,3 +1,5 @@
 ## Next actions
 - Compare options in a generated HTML report.
+- Tune only the small decision surface in a local disposable editor.
+- Copy the exported diff before applying any workspace change.

sections.title: Use this editor shape when
items: The user needs to sort, toggle, or tune a small local decision surface.
The final output can be represented as Markdown, JSON, or diff.
The artifact can remain useful without saving hidden state.

evidence.label: HTML effectiveness editor cases
evidence.value: Ticket triage board, feature flag editor, and prompt tuner all end in copyable output.

verification.label: Mutation boundary
verification.detail: The fixture uses generated static HTML, copy buttons, tabs, code, diff, and actions; it does not depend on hidden storage or write APIs.

nextActions: Use this fixture when evaluating lightweight editor guidance.
Keep production UI, bundled apps, and slide decks routed to their adjacent skills.
